item.title: Good idea, bad execution
item.content:encoded:
Should I upvote? Maybe not… Should I downvote… Nah.

The containment procedure is excellent. It's extensive, professional and technical. Personally, not a big fan over the "containment should be longer than description" but it's just personal taste and it doesn't really affect my judgement. My problem is entirely something else. Let me nitpick:

The guide said not to make the Foundation looks foolish. Wasting resource IS foolish.
hide...

Even though D-Class are expendable criminals that can be thrown to death whenever needed, they're still resource, and there's limited amount of resource too. Took many years for human to become adult, and even so, not all of them are going to the death row. Needless termination of D-Class is equivalent on throwing breads away, when the entire factor only delivers several packages of breads to your facility, per years. That's not efficient. That's wasteful.

The second nitpick would be…

The Ethics Committee improvise something, but it's not what they should improvise.
hide...

They're the ETHICS Committee for god sake. The first thing that they would question, is why the Foundation decide to kill people needlessly. A lot of people, in constant period of time. Not only it's unethical, it is unnecessary. We know that the Foundation is "cold, not cruel" and "necessary evil" but that is if it's necessary. They did them for the better good.

This? The containment procedure is not only a waste of resource, but also ineffective.

Think logically; if several D-Class with low intellect, untrained, barely equipped, could successfully do a good job on pruning the fatberg, why not sent a properly trained unit? The SCP by itself is not THAT dangerous. It's not going to dissolve everything in an instant. A simple hazmat suit or alike would drastically increase survival rate AND effectiveness of the containment.

It's a win-win situation with double benefit. You didn't waste life (and did not let the SCP commit mass murder which makes a containment kinda pointless) and you contain the SCP better.

And the final nitpick…. Sorry for being so blunt, but honestly….

This is a waste of a great idea.
hide...

The idea that an uncontrollable growing animal tissue IN the sewer is a good horror material. You manage to makes it unique by making it looks like that, instead of containing this piece of disgusting abomination, you take care of it instead, like a flower in the garden. That's sick, especially considering how revolting the entire thing is. That's a genuinely good horror material. It belongs here.

….But then?

Like what everyone else said, it's boring.

Yes, "boring" is subjective, but it's still a negative experience.

There's thousands of SCP here. To be successful, one should be interesting.

The problem? Yours IS interesting, but that interest, doesn't show, at all.

The idea is gold, the execution is poor.

If the idea is to makes something disgusting, there's countless of fatberg image on Google, some of them are even on Wikipedia, so you can use them here. A picture speak thousands words.

The SCP by itself is disgusting, disturbing, and that should BE the selling points.

Like what many said here: The entry is not interesting, and it leaves underwhelming impact.

Frankly, I rarely ever critiqued something, but I feel like I really need to do it for this one. I hope this doesn't comes as harsh, I don't want to be harsh. I also hope this doesn't comes as discouraging or negative, as I am always try to be supportive and positive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is literally The Formless Flesh from Darkest Dungeon, and yeah, I know that this skip is older. I would upvote, but I'm fully against wasting precious resources - this D-class aren't growing on trees, y'know?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love that someone tried to tell most of the story in the containment procedures, rather than description or addenda. This is a good example of pushing the format.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, there definitely could be a better explanation of why this thing needs to be contained the way it is. Even if it was a slight hint that this was set in BrightDuckman's low-tech verse would have been fine. I'm not icky to personnel being sent off to die (no offense to those that do take issue with it), but one can't help but feel that there's a ton of other methods that are simply more efficient in this case.

I like the fact that it's containment centric, though. +0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think doing some trimming in the CP before getting to the pull-quote about the "fatburg" would do a lot of good. For example, the bit about the biopsies could either be moved to the end, or eliminated entirely. Ditto for the line about the 10L of what is, for all intents and purposes to the reader, a completely random chemical.
It is always a fine line between building some suspense and stimulating the reader's imagination into thinking about what this could possibly be, to confusing the reader to the point that they lose interest. The "fatburg" is a nice clue as to what we're talking about, but it just comes too late, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like that theory… I suppose the question that still remains is "Why the bloody hell would they do that?", but perhaps the long-term containment plan is to slow or stop the growth of the thing by causing it to use up its nutrients on feeding and growing brain and bone tissue (brain tissue being metabolically expensive to maintain, and bone expensive to grow).

If that were the reason, though, I think it would be a better "stinger" at the end of the article to state that, if only obliquely. It feels like a satisfying-but-unpleasant justification for the whole thing, so I'm'a headcanon it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I agree with the comments upthread that mention how this gets a bit muddled, it actually held my attention. The containment procedures are a bit intense, and do well to build suspense. If you read into it enough, this is absolutely terrifying, but the thing is that I really shouldn't have to read into it THAT much. All the technobabble gets tedious, even if it is rooted in fact. But, as I said, I really like the idea of a giant pork mass slowly making its way to the outside, so you can have my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it possible they're feeding it D-class for the purpose of controlling the growth of the SCP to match the amount the two D-class can clear away before they're digested? I think that's what it's getting at by name checking the ethics committee and then mentioning cultures slow their rate of expansion to match the available nutrients. If that's what's being gotten at, it's a little bit too obfuscated I think.

Still, I like that you're giving the titular containment procedures their due here, and I have a special fondness for the ethics committee, so you get a well earned up vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoting this, partially because of aforementioned issues, but mostly just because I found disgusting purely for the sake of being disgusting. Worse, it was just boring, to the point where I just stopped caring halfway through. Plus, I honestly don't have a clue why they're both trying to hurt this thing and feed it at the same time. I'm aware that this may get a chorus of "secure contain protect not destroy destroy destroy" thrown at me, but this is a danger to containment and most likely would use far less resources in just killing it, not to mention far less wasted life.

Edit: just because I took a second look at the object class, why isn't this Keter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got some hints of this which is why I upvoted regardless of the abovementioned issues, though, I was wondering whether the author has something to say.

The lab-growing of a specimen still stands though - hell, you could get away with the same experimentation by feeding it D-Class at a lesser volume, as they'd be a higher proportion of its mass that way. I suspect what the project is on about anyways is removing useless generated tissues and then forcing the thing to regenerate using the D-class as material, which is what leads to the developments observed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and the most disgusting part, to me, is that there is nothing to suggest that anyone involved on the site with the SCP has a problem with committing mass murder.

Half of what the ethics committee is about, really.
Shuffling people about to find them jobs where they'll be obedient little cogs and won't complain or cause trouble, one way or another.
I suspect the people who ended up on this one were a liability elsewhere through cavalier attitudes to, among other things, D-Class lives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If this is true, there's going to need to be some more serious hints as to what this thing is/wants before it gets my upvote. Yeah, subtlety and all, but there needs to be something that suggests there is an underlying idea, not just giving the author the benefit of the doubt that they didn't just stop writing and say "This'll be so mysterious!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nothing about letting a random anomaly commit mass murder should be integral for an article. Foundation doesn't destroy anomalies, but there has to be a limit, and nothing about this thing suggests anything worth saving. It's a needless ugly waste that stupid people use to murder innocent people with. Nothing about it suggests there is anything deeper or sinister at work here, nothing about it suggests this is the only option available, and the most disgusting part, to me, is that there is nothing to suggest that anyone involved on the site with the SCP has a problem with committing mass murder.

If the focus had been on the ethics of letting an anomaly commit mass murder in order to keep it contained because it was invincible or something, that would have been one thing, but the way it plays out now is more suited to a trashy grindhouse flick, without any of the action or tongue-in-cheek humor to cover the wanton mass murder

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fatberg meets long pork. Interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but it seems like most people need more of a shove in the right direction.

I automatically side against any SCP that involves the senseless and repeated killing of D-Class, much less an SCP that involves the senseless and repeated killing of D-Class that doesn't even help to contain the SCP. If anything, what you're saying involves the Foundation actively helping an anomaly to grow and the only way to do that is to expend countless and unnecessary D-Class. This is actually worse than the Foundation merely containing it by killing countless D-Class, because at least the latter involves actually containing it.

So this article is also, from my perspective, making the Foundation look incompetent. Since we don't see things from the O5's position, I literally can not come to any conclusion besides "the Foundation has gone incompetent and dense-headed".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See my comment upthread. I suspect the D-Class being eaten is integral to the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one's pretty odd. A lot of people are complaining about how we're throwing D-Class into a meaty garbage disposal, but I think something is being missed:

-The Ethics Committee has prevented anyone from knowing what's going on down there for… Some reason.

-The D-Class are intentionally given corrodable/biodegradable materials. Because… We want the SCP to not get a tummy-ache?

-As you said, very inefficient methods are being used on this thing. However, as the rest of the article seems quite well-thought-out, this would seem to be by design.

-Since the Foundation has undertaken containment and started feeding D-Class to the SCP, the meaty-sheety thing has been growing in complexity by adding neural, sensory and structural (bone) tissue.

The Foundation's up to something here. This is one of those SCPs where it's obvious that the reader doesn't have O5 access, and is almost certainly being lied to by those more in the know. This article just doesn't shove it in your face like most do, which is refreshing, but it seems like most people need more of a shove in the right direction.

I'm not certain what the underlying secret of why the Foundation is actually feeding people to this thing is (or allowing them to be integrated into it, possibly still conscious afterward), and I think just a couple of textual hints in that direction would've put this article over the top for me. However, as it is, it's still a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, but Atreus… eh… don't kill so many D-Classes, please, it feels like a waste of resources.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Special Containment Procedure longer than the description? How deliciously absurd!

I like it. I've read about the sewers of Amsterdam and they are just perfect for this sort of thing. Always a fan of horrors hidden just below our notice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Dutch fuck up again. Is it a coincidence a Dutch guy wrote this? I think not!

Anyway, fucking horrying. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OMG YESS HAVE MY UPVOTES +1

Seriously though, I was waiting for someone to make a fatberg SCP. Question though - why don't we limit its growth using chemical agents? After all, we could just lab-grow a specimen for research purposes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let me see if I get what's happening…

spoilers maybe?
...or I could be wrong...

Someone at Utrecht University (probably the 'Van Koot' and 'et al' that wrote "Meat Sheets: Metastable Proliferation of Wild Boar muscle cells in vitro") was researching the idea of growing pork that doesn't require slaughtering livestock. But some material was improperly disposed of, and so now there's miles of pig musculature growing in the sewers, and its starting to grow bones, nerves and eyes?

Huh… that's… pretty horrifying…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There doesn't seem to be any rhyme or reason to this other than wasting D-Class lives, and despite the lengthy containment procedures it boils down to "throw people at it until it dies or we run out of people." This could be said with far fewer words, and other than the initial bizarreness factor, nothing in this article is memorable or interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tentative upvote because the concept is kind of cool, if a little too "tell don't show." Reminds me of the Cathedral of Flesh from Vampire: The Masquerade.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All that effort in the containment procedures just to lead to "we're stupid, so we let an anomaly murder people every few months"

Seriously, what even is the point of all this beyond "lets murder people by throwing it at a weird thing"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a giant pig monster. Lining the sewers of Amsterdam. That must be fed D-Class as its growth is stinted with shovels.

Holy fuck. Here, take an upvote. This is precisely the sort of conspiracy theory fuel this website needs more of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found it to be clear enough.

It's not the most engaging piece or storyful article on the site, but I'm going to upvote it on the principle that I like seeing more articles that are Containment-driven, even if I can't pull them off myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You bury your skip so hard, that I can't even be interested by the time I get to the description. And when I do get to the description, I feel immensely underwhelmed.

EDIT: Also, all this wall of information does is obfuscate what this is or what it's trying to do, so you leave me immensely confused. Even if this was cleared up, the other problems persist in how buried this thing is.
